steps.title: Set up the substitution
steps.content: We're given the linear model $y = 0.67x + 2.6$ that predicts the number of larvae based on the number of worker ants $x$. This colony has $x = 58$ worker ants, so we substitute this value into the equation.

steps.title: Calculate the predicted number of larvae
steps.content:
$$y = 0.67(58) + 2.6$$
$$y = 38.86 + 2.6$$
$$y = 41.46$$
The predicted number of larvae is approximately 41.46, which is closest to 41 among the choices. That matches choice A — A is correct.
